purpose: Record manual and assisted accessibility evidence for the task-first v0.6.0 UI release without storing personal speech data or binary screenshots.

localAutomatedEvidence.hostLimitation: Linux container cannot run Windows NVDA, Microsoft Edge, macOS Safari, or VoiceOver; those rows are documented as accepted external limitations with substitute local evidence.

checks.description: Axe-core scan on required route/state fixtures has zero critical or serious violations.
Tab order reaches primary navigation and route controls with no keyboard trap and no obscured focused element in Chromium.
Forced-colours mode preserves focus visibility, labels, route operation, and no horizontal overflow on required routes.
A 200% root text-scale smoke confirms required routes remain operable without page-level horizontal overflow.
Visible live-region text is short, status-oriented, and does not wrap the transcript editor or full route contents.
Required primary actions remain keyboard-focusable controls or labelled file inputs; tooltip/hover text is not required for completion.

assistiveTechnologyMatrix.environment: Chrome-compatible keyboard-only path in Playwright Chromium on Linux

acceptedLimitations: Microsoft Edge is not available in this Linux container. Edge keyboard verification must be repeated on Windows before final release sign-off; no Edge-specific code path is introduced by v0.6.0.

assistiveTechnologyMatrix.environment: NVDA with current Chrome on Windows
assistiveTechnologyMatrix.requiredByPlan: NVDA with current Chrome on Windows
acceptedLimitations: NVDA and Windows are not available in this Linux container. The matrix records local semantic, keyboard, axe, and live-region evidence; a human NVDA/Chrome pass remains external release evidence.

assistiveTechnologyMatrix.environment: NVDA with current Edge on Windows
assistiveTechnologyMatrix.requiredByPlan: NVDA with current Edge on Windows
acceptedLimitations: NVDA, Windows, and Edge are not available in this Linux container. The matrix records local semantic, keyboard, axe, and live-region evidence; a human NVDA/Edge pass remains external release evidence.

assistiveTechnologyMatrix.environment: VoiceOver with Safari on macOS for general UI compatibility
assistiveTechnologyMatrix.requiredByPlan: VoiceOver with Safari on macOS general UI compatibility
acceptedLimitations: macOS Safari and VoiceOver are not available in this Linux container. The matrix records local semantic, keyboard, axe, and live-region evidence; a human VoiceOver/Safari pass remains external release evidence.

assistiveTechnologyMatrix.requiredByPlan: Windows High Contrast or forced-colours mode
acceptedLimitations: Playwright forced-colours emulation is not a substitute for every Windows High Contrast theme; manual Windows confirmation remains useful before release tagging.

assistiveTechnologyMatrix.environment: Chromium 200% root text-scale smoke with narrow viewport checks
assistiveTechnologyMatrix.requiredByPlan: 200% browser zoom and operating-system text scaling
acceptedLimitations: The local check applies 200% text scaling and reflow assertions in Chromium; final release can add OS-specific text-scaling confirmation on Windows/macOS.

requiredOutcomes: zero critical or serious axe-core violations on required route fixtures
no keyboard trap on route-level Tab traversal
no focused element obscured by sticky header, fixed bottom navigation, dialogs, or popovers
no required action available only by hover or tooltip
live regions announce concise state changes rather than full transcript or route contents
external AT limitations are explicit and cannot be mistaken for completed Windows/macOS manual passes
